title: Shared types and enums
description: Types referenced by multiple agent schemas. Centralised so the vocabulary stays consistent.

ReportType.description: The analytical shape of a request. A1 assigns; downstream agents use this to pick block patterns and components.

AnalysisLevel.description: Five-level complexity spectrum A1 classifies every question on. L1 = single fact (one metric, one window). L2 = ranking or period-over-period comparison. L3 = multi-surface descriptive ('how is X doing') — parallel basket across surfaces. L4 = single-metric diagnostic ('why did X change') — triggers A2's universal RCA playbook expansion. L5 = compound, open-ended, un-mapped, or single-event multi-pillar. No level implies the system will interpret causes; the level only changes the data-collection depth/shape at A2. Cross-field rule: L1-L3 imply interpretation_request=false; L4-L5 imply interpretation_request=true.

AmbiguityFlag.description: Closed enum of ambiguities A1 can flag. Extending requires schema version bump.

Source.description: Data source identifier. Tools registered per deployment.

SentinelKind.description: Classification of GA4/GSC sentinel values so A6 can render them distinctly.

Status.description: Execution status used by A4 and downstream.

FailureKind.description: Classification of A4 failures. Drives retry vs surface decisions in the orchestrator.

SubQuestionId.description: Stable identifier for a sub-question across the pipeline. Pattern: sq_N where N is a positive integer.

QueryId.description: Stable identifier for a single query within a sub-question. Pattern: q_<sub>_<n> or descriptive (e.g. 'current', 'comparison_baseline').

BlockId.description: Stable identifier for a data block produced by A5.

DateString.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD. Used for absolute date_range values.

RelativeDateRange.description: Named relative date range. Resolved to absolute dates by A4 at execution time.

DateRange.description: Date range. After A3 resolution, status must be 'resolved'.

MappingChoice.description: A user-named term that has multiple catalog candidates. A2 emits; A3 resolves (clarification or default).

user_term.description: The term as the user wrote it.

expression.description: For filter mappings, the filter expression form.

preferred.description: Optional hint at the most likely choice. A3 must not auto-apply this; the registry decides.

DefaultCandidate.description: A field A1 flagged as missing that A3 may fill from the defaults registry.

registry_recommended.description: If the defaults registry has a recommended default for this field, surface it here. A3 still gates application.

FeasibilityFailure.description: A2 emits when a sub-question cannot be expressed in the available catalog. A3 routes to NEEDS_CLARIFICATION.

proxy_candidates.description: When reason='no_direct_concept', list of proxy investigations the user can choose (per bot-likely trace).

Disclosure.description: Factual user-facing note. Renderable in A6 verbatim. NEVER interpretive or causal.

AppliedDefault.description: Record of a default A3 applied automatically.

CarriedForward.description: A resolution from a prior turn carried into this one. Always surfaces as a disclosure.

source.description: Sticky-carryover key. resolved_metric_match means the prior turn resolved the same catalog metric (not necessarily the same user_term — see Phase-1 finding on 'engagement' vs 'engagement rate').

ScopeCues.description: Explicit entities the user named in their question. Preserved as-written, not normalised.

time.description: As written by the user, e.g. 'last week', 'in April', null if unsaid.

direction.description: User-named direction word for trend/change (e.g. 'dropping', 'rising'). Null if unsaid.

ComparisonIntent.description: Captured when the user asked for a comparison. Null when no comparison was named.
